topic: Agentic AI for reliable academic literature review and hallucination mitigation in large language models
final_review:
The integration of Agentic AI in academic literature review has the potential to enhance the reliability and accuracy of research findings (Kasneci et al., 2023). To mitigate the risk of hallucination in large language models, it is essential to develop explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) models that can provide transparent and interpretable results (Barredo Arrieta et al., 2019). The application of AI in higher education has been increasingly explored, with a focus on its potential to improve learning outcomes and enhance the educational experience (Zawacki‐Richter et al., 2019).

Recent studies have investigated the use of AI in educational settings, highlighting both the opportunities and challenges associated with this technology (Kasneci et al., 2023; Rudolph et al., 2023). The development of Agentic AI models that can engage in reliable academic literature review requires a multidisciplinary approach, incorporating insights from fields such as computer science, education, and social science (Dwivedi et al., 2019). Furthermore, it is essential to consider the ethical implications of AI adoption in academic settings, including issues related to bias, fairness, and transparency (Barredo Arrieta et al., 2019).

The use of large language models in academic settings also raises concerns about the potential for these models to generate false or misleading information (Dwivedi et al., 2023). By addressing these challenges and developing more advanced AI models, it is possible to create more reliable and effective academic literature review systems that can support researchers and educators in their work. The role of educators in AI-driven academic settings is an important area of consideration, with implications for the future of education (Zawacki‐Richter et al., 2019).

References:
Barredo Arrieta, A., Díaz-Rodríguez, N., Del Ser, J. (2019)
Dwivedi, Y. K., Hughes, L., Ismagilova, E. (2019)
Dwivedi, Y. K., Kshetri, N., Hughes, L. (2023)
Kasneci, E., Seßler, K., Küchemann, S. (2023)
Rudolph, J., Tan, S., Tan, S. (2023)
Zawacki‐Richter, O., Marín, V. I., Bond, M. (2019)
